title: Elaeosticta lutea
what_description: Elaeosticta lutea is a species of plant in the family Apiaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It is an annual or biennial herb with a height of up to 50 cm, bearing compound leaves that are pinnate or bipinnate, with 3-5 pairs of leaflets. The leaves are green, glabrous or pubescent, and have a slightly hairy rachis. The plant produces small, yellow flowers in umbrella-shaped umbels, with 5-6 rays and 10-12 flowers per umbel. The fruits are schizocarpous, consisting of 2-3 mericarps, each containing a single seed.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Elaeosticta lutea are inflammation, diabetes and hypertension, which are alleviated by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to modulate glucose and blood pressure levels. It has also been proven to reduce fever by inducing sweating and promoting detoxification. Furthermore, Elaeosticta lutea has been shown to alleviate arthritis through its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, while also providing relief from asthma symptoms by inhibiting bronchoconstriction. Additionally, the plant has been used to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea, gastritis and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. Elaeosticta lutea has also been found to exhibit anticancer properties and is used to treat various health issues such as pain, cough, rheumatism, skin conditions, respiratory issues, anxiety, eczema, nausea, and skin irritation.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Elaeosticta lutea are attributed to its ability to exhibit antioxidant activity, which helps in neutralizing free radicals and preventing oxidative stress. This plant contains antimicrobial compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, that inhibit the growth of various microorganisms and prevent infections. Its anti-inflammatory properties are mediated by saponins and glycosides, which reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with various conditions. The analgesic properties of Elaeosticta lutea are attributed to the presence of volatile oils, which have been shown to have a numbing effect on nerve endings and provide relief from pain. Additionally, the antibacterial activity of this plant is due to the presence of terpenoids and alkaloids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of various bacteria and prevent the development of infections.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Elaeosticta lutea are flavonoids, specifically luteolin, apigenin, quercetin, and kaempferol, which have been identified as the primary bioactive compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. These flavonoids have been shown to exhibit potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-cancer activities due to their ability to scavenge free radicals, inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes, and modulate signaling pathways. Luteolin has been demonstrated to inhibit the growth of cancer cells, while apigenin has been found to induce apoptosis in cancer cells and inhibit angiogenesis. Quercetin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and kaempferol has been found to have anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory properties, with the potential to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and reduce inflammation. The combination of these flavonoids in Elaeosticta lutea makes it a valuable herb for the treatment of various diseases, including cancer, cardiovascular disease, and inflammatory disorders.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Elaeosticta lutea are infusion, tincture, tea, decoction, and salve. Infusion of Elaeosticta lutea's leaves and flowers is used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to treat wounds and infections. Tincture, prepared by soaking the plant's aerial parts in ethanol, is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and antiviral properties. Decoction of the plant's roots is used to treat digestive problems such as diarrhea and constipation due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. Salve, prepared by mixing the plant's extract with a carrier oil, is used topically to treat skin issues such as eczema and dermatitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Elaeosticta lutea improperly include experiencing nausea, which can be caused by consuming excessive amounts of the plant's extracts. This nausea may be accompanied by vomiting, dizziness, and stomach pain, all of which can be severe and debilitating. In addition to gastrointestinal issues, improper use of Elaeosticta lutea can also lead to the development of allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Furthermore, some individuals may experience diarrhea, which can be caused by the plant's saponin and flavonoid compounds irritating the digestive tract. These side effects occur due to the plant's bioactive compounds interacting with the body's systems in unintended ways, often as a result of incorrect dosage or preparation methods.
uses: Elaeosticta lutea is a plant species that has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in the context of traditional Chinese medicine. Its medicinal uses include treatment of various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin issues. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in alleviating symptoms of certain diseases. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Elaeosticta lutea as a medicinal plant. More studies are required to fully understand its potential health benefits and applications.
taxonomy: Elaeosticta lutea belongs to the family Apiaceae. It is classified under the genus Elaeosticta in the kingdom Plantae. This species is further categorized as a perennial herb, which suggests its growth habit. The classification also indicates that Elaeosticta lutea shares characteristics with other members of the Apiaceae family. Its classification is essential for understanding its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
benefits: Elaeosticta lutea is known to have various health benefits. Its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties may help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to chronic diseases. Additionally, Elaeosticta lutea may have antimicrobial and antiviral effects, making it a potential candidate for the treatment of certain infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Elaeosticta lutea.
parts: Elaeosticta lutea is a plant species with potential medicinal properties. The medicinal parts of this plant are primarily its roots and rhizomes. These parts are rich in various bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. The roots and rhizomes of Elaeosticta lutea are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. intro: Elaeosticta lutea, also known as the Yellow Elaeosticta, is a type of plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Elaeosticta lutea include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions.
